metaTitle: Tweet Generator — Fake Tweet Maker Online (Free & Private)
metaDescription: Create realistic-looking fake tweets instantly in your browser. Customize name, username, photo, stats and more. 100% private — your data never leaves your device.

howto.description: Enter a name, username, tweet text, and stats like likes, retweets, and views.
Optionally upload a profile photo from your device. Nothing is sent to any server.
See your fake tweet update instantly as you type.
Click Save to download the tweet as a PNG image directly to your device.

why: 100% private — your photos and data never leave your browser
Realistic X (Twitter) UI with light and dark theme support
Real-time preview updates as you type
Custom avatar, name, username, stats and more
Works completely offline after the initial page load
No watermarks, no sign-up, completely free forever

faq.q: Is this tool safe to use with my photos?
faq.a: Yes. Everything runs entirely in your browser. Your photos and personal data are never uploaded to any server.

faq.q: Can I use this on my phone?
faq.a: Absolutely. The tool is fully optimized for mobile browsers including Safari on iOS and Chrome on Android.

faq.q: Will the generated image have a watermark?
faq.a: No. The downloaded image is completely clean with no watermarks or branding.

faq.q: Can I make a dark mode tweet?
faq.a: Yes! You can switch between light and dark theme to match the look you want.

faq.q: Is it okay to use this for pranks?
faq.a: This tool is intended for fun and creative purposes only. Please do not use generated images to spread misinformation or harm others.

faq.q: What image formats are supported for the avatar?
faq.a: You can upload JPG, PNG, and WebP images for the avatar.

troubleshooting: Image not downloading? Ensure you've granted 'Save Image' permissions in your mobile browser.
Avatar looking blurry? Try uploading a square image with at least 400x400 pixels for best results.
Font looking different? The tool uses system fonts that mimic the official X (Twitter) typography.
proTips: Switch to Dark Mode to create 'night owl' style tweets that stand out in light-themed feeds.
Use realistic numbers for likes and retweets to make your prank or meme look more authentic.
Try adding emojis to your tweet text – our generator fully supports the latest emoji sets.
